lastAction: ESC-003 resolved (CLI-argument-injection guard, FINDING-SEC-001); fresh test-designer added TEST-283..290 (RED, tests gate re-earned); fresh implementer added rejectFlagLike() guard to src/shared/orky-action-validate.ts (npm test 140/140 files, 1033/1033 tests; npm run build clean; implement gate re-earned). Review fan-out resumed: security lens re-verified FINDING-SEC-001 now resolved (no new findings); quality lens filed 5 open LOW/MEDIUM findings (FINDING-QUAL-001..005, none blocking); devils-advocate lens filed FINDING-DA-001 (HIGH, contract_violation:true -- REQ-015's per-featureDir OrkyActionQueue is keyed by the RAW featureDir string while REQ-004's root-allowlist accepts case/slash-divergent spellings via normalizeProjectRoot, so two concurrent mutating actions on the same physical feature via differently-cased projectRoot spellings bypass serialization and can race gatekeeper's non-atomic state.json read-modify-write) plus FINDING-DA-002/003 (MEDIUM/LOW, open, non-blocking). FINDING-DA-001 verified independently by the coordinator and escalated as ESC-004; human approved keying OrkyActionQueue.run() by normalizeProjectRoot(featureDir) at all 3 call sites in orky-action-dispatcher.ts. Looped back to tests to add regression coverage for the fix before re-implementing (networking/enterprise-arch/codex-cross-check lenses still not yet dispatched this review cycle -- will resume after the fix re-earns tests/implement).
